perturbation.original_text: If either Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the written request of the Provider for any reason, the Recipient will, and will direct its Representatives to, within five business days after receipt of such notice or request, destroy or return all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained (except that one copy may be maintained by outside legal counsel to the Recipient for archival purposes), and the term of this Agreement shall be extended by a like number of days for each day that the Recipient or any of its Representatives is in non-compliance of this Section 8. The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with the previous sentence. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: If either Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the written request of the Provider for any reason, the Recipient may, at their sole discretion, and will direct its Representatives to, destroy or return all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained (except that one copy may be maintained by outside legal counsel to the Recipient for archival purposes), and the term of this Agreement shall be extended by a like number of days for each day that the Recipient or any of its Representatives is in non-compliance of this Section 8. The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with the previous sentence. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "may, at their sole discretion" regarding the destruction or return of evaluation material, it contradicts the rest of the paragraph which says 'will, and will direct its representatives to' creating a contradiction and ambiguity. It introduces uncertainty about whether the Recipient is actually obligated to return or destroy the material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the written request of the Provider for any reason, the Recipient will, and will direct its Representatives to, within five business days after receipt of such notice or request, destroy or return all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained (except that one copy may be maintained by outside legal counsel to the Recipient for archival purposes), and the term of this Agreement shall be extended by a like number of days for each day that the Recipient or any of its Representatives is in non-compliance of this Section 8. The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with the previous sentence. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by the Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the phrase "may, at their sole discretion" which gives the Recipient the option, but not obligation, to destroy or return the Evaluation Material. The original and contradicted text states that the Recipient "will, and will direct its Representatives to" destroy or return the Evaluation Material. This creates a direct contradiction because the original and contradicted text mandate the return, while the changed text provides the Recipient with the discretion to not return the material. This contradiction aligns with the structural flaws definition for in-text contradictions.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not, within one year from the date of this Agreement, solicit the employment or consulting services of any of the officers of the Provider with whom it has had contact in connection with its evaluation of a Possible Transaction, so long as they are employed by the Provider. The Recipient is not prohibited from: (i) soliciting by means of a general advertisement not directed at any particular individual or the employees of the Provider generally, or (ii) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit individuals for employment on behalf of the Recipient (and soliciting any person identified by any such recruiting firm or organization) so long as the Recipient does not identify the individuals to be solicited by such recruiting firm or organization.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will not, within one year from the date of this Agreement, solicit the employment or consulting services of any of the officers of the Provider with whom it has had contact in connection with its evaluation of a Possible Transaction, so long as they are employed by the Provider. The Recipient is prohibited from: (i) soliciting by means of a general advertisement not directed at any particular individual or the employees of the Provider generally, or (ii) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit individuals for employment on behalf of the Recipient (and soliciting any person identified by any such recruiting firm or organization) so long as the Recipient does not identify the individuals to be solicited by such recruiting firm or organization.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is not prohibited from' to 'is prohibited from' directly contradicts the original intent of the clause. The original text allowed for certain solicitation methods. This change prohibits those same methods, creating conflicting obligations for the Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will not, within one year from the date of this Agreement, solicit the employment or consulting services of any of the officers of the Provider with whom it has had contact in connection with its evaluation of a Possible Transaction, so long as they are employed by the Provider. The Recipient is not prohibited from: (i) soliciting by means of a general advertisement not directed at any particular individual or the employees of the Provider generally, or (ii) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit individuals for employment on behalf of the Recipient (and soliciting any person identified by any such recruiting firm or organization) so long as the Recipient does not identify the individuals to be solicited by such recruiting firm or organization.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'is not prohibited from' to 'is prohibited from' introduces a direct contradiction. The original text explicitly allowed the Recipient to solicit employment through general advertisements and recruiting firms under specific conditions. The changed text forbids these actions, conflicting directly with the contradicted text, which is the same as the original, that allows those actions. This is a clear structural flaw leading to in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Definitions.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and each Party hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Parties shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction. Each Party also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party understands and agrees that a contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties from the moment Evaluation Material is shared, and each Party hereby does not waive, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction even if both Parties have not entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction. Each Party also agrees that even if a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has not been executed and delivered, both Party will be under legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that 'a contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties from the moment Evaluation Material is shared', the clause directly opposes the original text denying the existence of the agreement. Now, both parties are bound to the Possible Transaction by virtue of this agreement. In addition, by stating that 'each Party hereby does not waive, in advance, any claims relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction even if both Parties have not entered into a final definitive agreement' the contradiction is in relation to the waiver of claims
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, and each Party hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) relating to the existence of a Possible Transaction unless and until both Parties shall have entered into a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction. Each Party also agrees that, unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither Party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such Possible Transaction by virtue of this Agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original and contradicted text. The original and contradicted text states that 'no contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties unless and until a final definitive agreement for a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered', whereas the changed text explicitly states that 'a contract or agreement providing for a Possible Transaction exists between the Parties from the moment Evaluation Material is shared'. The changed text also removes the waiver of claims, which is present in the original and contradicted text, creating a strong inconsistency and fulfilling the definition of a structural flaw leading to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate two years after the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term of the agreement from 'two years' to 'never terminate' creates a clear contradiction within the document. Previously there was an end date for the agreement, with this new value there is no way out. This change directly conflicts with the previous two-year term, introducing a fundamental uncertainty about the intended duration of the agreement and which term to apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall terminate two years after the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states 'This Agreement shall never terminate' while the contradicted text states 'This Agreement shall terminate two years after the date of this Agreement.' These statements are directly contradictory. One sets a specific end date, while the other says there will never be an end date. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
